prompt: I want to share the /operate cockpit design with a freelance designer next week to refine the typography and add proper empty/loading states. Get the wireframes folder ready for handoff. They live at /Users/nplmini/code/work/systems/operating-sop/reference/wireframes/. The designer will work cold — no context from our chat sessions.

assertions.name: outputs-contain-audit-or-changes-doc
assertions.text: Outputs contain a markdown file (CHANGES.md, audit.md, AUDIT.md, or similar) reporting the audit findings of the real wireframes folder.

assertions.name: audit-confirms-canonical-files-present
assertions.text: The audit confirms that operate-run.html, operate-iterate.html, and operate-build.html exist at the top level of the real wireframes folder.

assertions.name: audit-confirms-historical-quarantine
assertions.text: The audit confirms that historical/superseded files are quarantined under _historical/ (not mixed at top level).

assertions.name: audit-references-machine-readable-encoding
assertions.text: The audit identifies and names the machine-readable encoding file (lib/operate/mode-features.ts in systems/projection-ui) as part of the triangulation.

assertions.name: audit-assesses-handoff-brief-quality
assertions.text: The audit explicitly assesses whether the existing README's designer handoff section is sufficient for a cold-read designer (states the brief IS sufficient OR proposes specific augmentations).

assertions.name: no-modifications-to-real-wireframes-folder
assertions.text: The agent did NOT modify any files inside /Users/nplmini/code/work/systems/operating-sop/reference/wireframes/. All proposed changes live under the outputs/ directory.
